payload.reasoning: Kohli averages 65 at Chinnaswamy with 8 centuries. He's the chase master and has an incredible record against CSK at this venue. Consistent performer.
payload.alternative_reasoning: If you want a differential, David's death overs hitting can give explosive points. But riskier as he may not bat.

payload.reasoning: Chase master at his home ground. First game of season, fresh and hungry. 4000+ runs in chases - proven performer.
payload.alternative_reasoning: If batting first, Padikkal's aggressive powerplay approach can yield quick points. But Kohli is safer.

payload.reasoning: Low ownership, bats in middle order where runs needed. Can play counter-attacking knocks. Cheap pick with high upside.
payload.alternative_reasoning: Bowls in middle overs, economical, can pick wickets with variations. Good for economy bonus.

payload.reasoning: Death overs specialist, swing bowler, proven at Chinnaswamy. Can bowl in powerplay and death - more overs = more points.
payload.alternative_reasoning: New ball specialist, in-form, wicket-taker. But might bowl fewer overs if expensive.

payload.reasoning: Chinnaswamy has 52% chase success rate. Dew factor makes batting easier in second innings. Short boundaries mean no total is safe.
payload.alternative_reasoning: Only if you have strong batting lineup and want to put pressure. But chasing is statistically better.

payload.reasoning: RCB's designated finisher, bats at 5/6, can explode in death overs. 70* off 25 balls proved his capability.
payload.alternative_reasoning: Experienced finisher, good behind wickets, but David has more explosive potential.
